promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: SLCO1B1 encodes OATP1B1, a hepatic organic anion uptake transporter. This bundle emphasizes hepatic statin uptake, bile-acid and drug transport, transporter pharmacogenetics, and myopathy-risk biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local SLCO1B1 bundle is transporter pharmacogenomics oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: SLCO1B1 variants can alter or tag hepatic statin uptake, bile-acid and drug transport, transporter pharmacogenetics, and myopathy-risk biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: SLCO1B1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a hepatic statin uptake, bile-acid and drug transport, transporter pharmacogenetics, and myopathy-risk biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for SLCO1B1.
Because SLCO1B1 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.
Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against hepatic statin uptake, bile-acid and drug transport, transporter pharmacogenetics, and myopathy-risk biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use SLCO1B1 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for hepatic statin uptake, bile-acid and drug transport, transporter pharmacogenetics, and myopathy-risk biology.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a SLCO1B1 transporter pharmacogenomics thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to hepatic statin uptake, bile-acid and drug transport, transporter pharmacogenetics, and myopathy-risk biology and Statin-associated myopathy context, Hepatic transporter pharmacogenomics. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs4149056 (SLCO1B1 c.521T>C) is curated as a SLCO1B1 marker for Statin-associated myopathy context, Hepatic transporter pharmacogenomics. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the hepatic statin uptake, bile-acid and drug transport, transporter pharmacogenetics, and myopathy-risk biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag hepatic statin uptake, bile-acid and drug transport, transporter pharmacogenetics, and myopathy-risk biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a SLCO1B1 pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs4149056 (SLCO1B1 c.521T>C) with SLCO1B1 methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
